programs.name: South Dakota Medicaid for Seniors and Disabled
programs.what_it_does: Provides medical coverage including checkups, dental, and vision care for eligible seniors and disabled individuals. Covers those requiring nursing home level of care or in adult foster care.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+, blind or disabled under 65, US citizens or qualified residents of South Dakota meeting income/resource limits (e.g., $2,000 individual resources).
programs.benefit_value: Free or low-cost coverage; income limit 300% SSI ($2,982/month in 2026) for nursing home level care.

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Options and Person Centered Excellence (HOPE) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Delivers home and community-based services to help seniors avoid nursing homes. Includes homemaker services, private duty nursing, adult day care, emergency response, meals, assisted living, and companion services.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ or disabled 18+ needing nursing home level care, US citizens/residents, resource limit $40,000 single/$45,000 couple, possible share-of-cost.
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive HCBS to support community living; home equity limit $752,000.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Not specifically named or detailed in SD sources; generally provides comprehensive medical and social services for frail seniors to stay in community.
programs.who_its_for: Frail seniors 55+ eligible for nursing home care; no SD-specific program confirmed in results.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Helps pay Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, and coinsurance. QMB covers full costs, SLMB/QI cover premiums.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors eligible for Medicare Part A, meeting federal income/asset limits ($9,660 single/$14,470 married).
programs.benefit_value: Covers out-of-pocket Medicare costs based on program.

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Provides food assistance benefits via EBT card for groceries. Administered through SD DSS as federal program.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors, US citizens/residents; specific SD details not in results.
programs.benefit_value: Monthly benefits scaled to income/household size (federal standard).

programs.name: Low Income Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Helps low-income households pay home heating costs. Provided through Community Action Programs.
programs.benefit_value: Heating cost payments based on income.

programs.name: Community Assistance Program Weatherization
programs.what_it_does: Offers weatherization services to improve energy efficiency for low-income homes. Part of SD Community Action Programs.

programs.name: Senior Health Information and Insurance Education (SHIINE)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free, unbiased, confidential counseling on Medicare, insurance, and health benefits. Funded federally.
programs.who_its_for: South Dakota seniors needing Medicare guidance.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Not SD-specific name in results; delivers meals to homebound seniors (typically via Area Agencies on Aging).

programs.name: Respite Care
programs.what_it_does: Offers temporary relief for caregivers through in-home services like personal care and homemaking. Part of HOPE Waiver options.
programs.who_its_for: Caregivers of seniors 65+ or disabled adults via HOPE Waiver.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Not detailed in SD-specific results; federal program for paid community service training for seniors.

programs.name: Legal Aid for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Not specifically named in results; general legal services via senior resources.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman
programs.what_it_does: Not detailed in results; advocates for residents in long-term care facilities.

programs.name: Senior Companions of South Dakota
programs.what_it_does: This program matches healthy volunteer seniors aged 55+ with older adults and individuals 21+ needing assistance to live independently. Volunteers provide free companionship, light housekeeping, meal preparation, transportation, and social activities like playing games or reminiscing.[1][2][6]
programs.who_its_for: Older adults and adults 21+ needing help to remain independent, especially in rural areas; no charge to clients.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific implementation sponsored by the Good Samaritan Society, emphasizing rural South Dakota communities with volunteer-driven, free in-home support not universally available across all states in this form.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Free services including 10-40 hours/week of assistance per client, preventing long-term care placement and reducing isolation; volunteers receive stipend and mileage.[1]

programs.name: Senior Box Program (CSFP)
programs.what_it_does: Administered statewide by Feeding South Dakota in partnership with the Department of Education, it provides monthly pre-packaged boxes of nutritious, non-perishable food. This supplements diets lacking key nutrients, allowing seniors to allocate funds to medicine and utilities.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 60+ living on limited income in all 66 South Dakota counties.[3]
programs.whats_unique: Statewide partnership delivers USDA CSFP exclusively across all counties via a local nonprofit, addressing senior hunger in rural areas more comprehensively than in states without such full-coverage administration.[3]
programs.benefit_value: One box of nutrient-supplementing food per month at no cost, valued for freeing up budget for essentials like housing and medications.[3]
